facts.text: The word 'set' has the highest number of definitions in the English language - over 430!
'I am' is the shortest complete sentence in the English language.
The dot over the letters 'i' and 'j' is called a tittle.
The word 'alphabet' comes from the first two letters of the Greek alphabet: alpha and beta.
English is the official language of 67 countries and 27 non-sovereign entities.
The longest word in English without a vowel is 'rhythms'.
'Strengths' is the longest word in the English language with only one vowel.
The word 'girl' was previously used to refer to a child of any gender.
About 4,000 words are added to the English dictionary every year.
The most common adjective in English is 'good'.
The most frequently used noun is 'time'.
The word 'checkmate' in chess comes from the Persian phrase 'shah mat', meaning 'the king is helpless'.
The letter 'e' is the most frequently used letter in English.
The least frequently used letter is 'z'.
The word 'triskaidekaphobia' means fear of the number 13.
'Bookkeeper' and 'bookkeeping' are the only words in English that have three consecutive double letters.
The word 'queue' is pronounced the same way even if you remove the last four letters.
Shakespeare invented over 1,700 words in the English language.
The word 'sorry' appears more often in British English than in American English.
The oldest word in English is 'town'.
The word 'robot' comes from the Czech word 'robota', meaning forced labor.
The phrase 'rule of thumb' is believed to come from an old English law that allowed a man to beat his wife with a stick no thicker than his thumb.
The word 'deadline' originally referred to a line around a prison beyond which prisoners would be shot.
The word 'muscle' comes from the Latin word meaning 'little mouse'.
The term 'volunteer' comes from the Latin word 'voluntarius', meaning 'of one's own free will'.
The word 'disaster' comes from Greek 'dis' (bad) and 'aster' (star) - literally 'bad star'.
The word 'panic' comes from the Greek god Pan, who was believed to cause sudden fear in travelers.
The word 'clue' comes from the Greek 'clew', meaning a ball of yarn.
The word 'sarcasm' comes from the Greek word 'sarkazein', meaning 'to tear flesh'.
The word 'quiz' was invented by a theater manager in 1791 as a bet.
The word 'hello' was invented by Thomas Edison.
The word 'news' comes from the first letters of the four directions: North, East, West, South.
The word 'laser' is an acronym for Light Amplification by Stimulated Emission of Radiation.
The word 'posh' originated as an acronym for 'Port Out, Starboard Home' on luxury ships.
The word 'gung-ho' comes from the Chinese word 'gong he', meaning 'work together'.
The word 'kangaroo' comes from the Aboriginal word 'gangurru'.
The word 'chocolate' comes from the Aztec word 'xocolatl'.
The word 'avocado' comes from the Aztec word 'ahuacatl', meaning testicle.
The word 'tomato' comes from the Aztec word 'tomatl'.
The word 'vanilla' comes from the Spanish word 'vainilla', meaning little pod.
The word 'coffee' comes from the Arabic word 'qahwah'.
The word 'tea' comes from the Chinese word 't'e'.
The word 'banana' comes from the Arabic word 'banan'.
The word 'orange' comes from the Sanskrit word 'naranga'.
The word 'lemon' comes from the Arabic word 'laymun'.
The word 'sugar' comes from the Sanskrit word 'sharkara'.
The word 'candy' comes from the Sanskrit word 'khanda'.
The word 'pirate' comes from the Greek word 'peirates', meaning one who attacks.
The word 'typewriter' can be typed using only the top row of a QWERTY keyboard.
The sentence 'The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog' uses every letter of the alphabet.
Honey never spoils. Archaeologists have found 3000-year-old honey in Egyptian tombs that was still edible.
Octopuses have three hearts and blue blood.
A group of flamingos is called a 'flamboyance'.
Bananas are berries, but strawberries are not.
A day on Venus is longer than a year on Venus.
The shortest war in history lasted 38 to 45 minutes between Britain and Zanzibar.
The Great Wall of China is not visible from space with the naked eye.
Napoleon was once attacked by a herd of rabbits.
A bolt of lightning contains enough energy to toast 100,000 slices of bread.
Cows have best friends and get stressed when separated.
The Hawaiian alphabet has only 12 letters.
A snail can sleep for three years.
The fingerprints of koalas are so similar to humans that they've been confused at crime scenes.
There are more stars in the universe than grains of sand on all of Earth's beaches.
A jiffy is an actual unit of time: 1/100th of a second.
The first oranges weren't orange - they were green.
Wombat poop is cube-shaped.
The inventor of the Pringles can is buried in one.
There's a planet made of diamond.
A cloud can weigh more than a million pounds.
The smell of freshly cut grass is actually a plant distress call.
Human teeth are the only part of the body that cannot heal themselves.
A shrimp's heart is located in its head.
The world's oldest known living tree is over 5,000 years old.
A blue whale's heart is so big that a small child could crawl through its arteries.
The first alarm clock could only ring at 4 a.m.
There are more possible iterations of a game of chess than atoms in the known universe.
A group of crows is called a 'murder'.
The name of all the continents end with the same letter they start with.
The longest time between two twins being born is 87 days.
The Sahara desert is the largest desert in the world - Antarctica is actually the largest.
The Spanish national anthem has no words.
The State of Nevada has more hotel rooms than the entire country of Australia.
The Australian dollar is made of plastic.
The world's largest pyramid is in Mexico, not Egypt.
The official language of the United States has never been declared English.
The cigarette lighter was invented before the match.
The first email was sent in 1971.
The first computer bug was an actual bug - a moth found in a Harvard computer in 1947.
The first domain name registered was symbolics.com.
The first 1GB hard drive was announced in 1980, weighed 550 pounds, and cost $40,000.
The first computer mouse was made of wood.
The first video game ever created was Tennis for Two in 1958.
The first cell phone was invented in 1973 and weighed 2.4 pounds.
The first text message was sent in 1992 and said 'Merry Christmas'.
The world's first website is still online at info.cern.ch.
The word 'WiFi' doesn't mean anything - it's a play on 'Hi-Fi'.
The average person will spend six months of their life waiting at red lights.
More people are killed annually by coconuts than by sharks.
The average person walks the equivalent of five times around the Earth in their lifetime.
